title: notodanthonia clelandii
intro: Notodanthonia clelandii, also known as Cleland's danthonia, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae, native to eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, Notodanthonia clelandii is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its numerous health benefits.
